doc_name: Integra Lifesciences hit with FDA warning letter over Boston facility

chunks.doc_name: Integra Lifesciences hit with FDA warning letter over Boston facility
chunks.text: [MassDevice](https://www.massdevice.com/) The Medical Device Business Journal — Medical Device News & Articles | MassDevice # Integra Lifesciences hit with FDA warning letter over Boston facility August 23, 2023 By [Chris Newmarker](https://www.massdevice.com/author/cnewmarker/) ![Integra Lifesciences logo](https://www.massdevice.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/12/integra-lifesciences-logo-300x195.png)The FDA says Integra Lifesciences (Nasdaq: IART) + has inadequately responded to violations that it found at the company’s TEI Biosciences plant in Boston. ![Integra Lifesciences logo](https://www.massdevice.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/12/integra-lifesciences-logo-300x195.png) Integra Lifesciences’ Boston facility makes extracellular bovine matrix products for tissue repair and regeneration, wound care and more. In May, the company [voluntarily recalled](https://www.sec.gov/ix?doc=/Archives/edgar/data/917520/000091752023000035/iart-20230522.htm) all tissue-related products made in Boston and distributed between March 1, 2018 and May 22, 2023. The recalled products included SurgiMend, PriMatrix, Revize and TissueMend. According to the company, an internal investigation found deviations with endotoxin testing. The result was a release of products with higher levels of endotoxins than permitted by the product specifications. Higher levels of endotoxins can induce an immune response, including a postoperative fever. The facility previously r[eceived an FDA warning letter in 2019](https://www.massdevice.com/fda-warns-integras-tei-biosciences-over-quality-validation-issues/). A new FDA warning letter — dated July 17 and posted on FDA’s website on Aug. 15 — is based on a new inspection from March 1 through May 17. It notes the recall as well as a company response dated June 8. The new letter lists four violations: On all four counts, the FDA says Integra Lifesciences’ response has not been adequate. ([Read the full warning letter here.](https://www.fda.gov/inspections-compliance-enforcement-and-criminal-investigations/warning-letters/integra-lifesciences-corporation-660960-07172023)) ### Integra Lifesciences says it takes the FDA’s concerns seriously In a statement shared with *MassDevice*, the company said it takes commitment to patient safety and product quality very seriously. The company is working closely with the FDA to fully address its concerns related to the Boston facility. Integra Lifesciences added: “We will not resume production until we have addressed the FDA concerns and are confident the facility meets the strictest regulatory and product quality standards, which we [anticipate will occur by the end of the calendar year.](https://www.massdevice.com/integra-lifesciences-expects-to-resume-manufacturing-in-boston/)” The FDA warning letter says Integra Lifesciences has until March 31, 2024, to provide initial certification by an outside consultant, with subsequent certifications provided March 31, 2025, and March 31, 2026. Filed Under: [Featured](https://www.massdevice.com/category/featured/), [Food & Drug Administration (FDA)](https://www.massdevice.com/category/regulatory_compliance/food_drug_administration_fda/), [News Well](https://www.massdevice.com/category/news_well/), [Regulatory/Compliance](https://www.massdevice.com/category/regulatory_compliance/), [Surgical](https://www.massdevice.com/category/surgical/), [Wound Care](https://www.massdevice.com/category/wound-care/) Tagged With: [Integra LifeSciences](https://www.massdevice.com/tag/integralifesciences/) ### More recent news ![](https://secure.gravatar.com/avatar/078ee426f5152e3df594df751f12c4bf12ac0455b869c2d71a62ec64b0ea748f?s=70&d=retro&r=g) #### About Chris Newmarker Chris Newmarker is the executive editor of WTWH Media life science's news websites and publications including MassDevice, Medical Design & Outsourcing and more. A professional journalist of 18 years, he is a veteran of UBM (now Informa) and The Associated Press whose career has taken him from Ohio to Virginia, New Jersey and, most recently, Minnesota. He’s covered a wide variety of subjects, but his focus over the past decade has been business and technology. He holds bachelor’s degrees in journalism and political science from Ohio State University.
